steps.title: Identify complementary acute angles
steps.content: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary, so $$X + Y = 90^\circ$$. Therefore $$Y = 90^\circ - X$$.

steps.title: Apply co‑function identity
steps.content: Using the co‑function identity $$\sin \theta = \cos(90^\circ - \theta)$$, substitute $$\theta = X$$: $$\sin X = \cos(90^\circ - X) = \cos Y$$. Thus $$\sin X$$ equals $$\cos Y$$, which corresponds to choice A. That matches choice A — A is correct.
